semantic.role: Manifest index file for ArchSpine Atlas, recording the current state of indexed source files and their associated documentation.
responsibilities: Maintain a registry of all indexed source files with content hashes for change detection.
Associate each source file with its documentation (Atlas entries) per locale.
Track sync metadata including last sync time, mode, and completeness of reverse index.
Provide a lightweight state snapshot for runtime and synchronization workflows.
invariants: contentHash must match the SHA-256 hash of the actual file content.
fileKind must be one of the allowed source types (e.g., 'source', 'test', 'config').
sourceExists must be true for files that currently exist on disk; false if deleted.
Each doc entry must have a valid locale code and a path within .spine/atlas/.
sync.indexedUnitCount must equal the number of entries in the 'files' object.

English.role: This manifest file acts as the central index for the ArchSpine Atlas system. It records every source file that has been indexed, along with its content fingerprint and links to its documentation in supported locales. It also stores synchronization checkpoint data used by the runtime to determine when a rescan is needed.

parameter_definitions.schemaVersion: Defines the version of the manifest schema. Updating this may break compatibility with older tools.
parameter_definitions.generatorVersion: Identifies the exact version of the ArchSpine tool that created this manifest. Useful for debugging and migration.
parameter_definitions.createdAt: Timestamp of initial creation. Helps trace the lifecycle of the index.
parameter_definitions.updatedAt: Timestamp of last modification. Used to detect stale state.
parameter_definitions.sync: A block containing synchronization metadata. Controls whether the runtime considers the index up-to-date or in need of a full rebuild.
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncAt: Records when the last sync operation completed. Used for incremental sync decisions.
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncMode: Indicates whether the last sync was 'full' or 'incremental'. Affects the trust level of the index.
parameter_definitions.sync.reverseIndexComplete: Boolean flag that tells the system whether the reverse index (mapping docs back to source files) has been fully built. If false, certain operations may be impaired.
parameter_definitions.sync.indexedUnitCount: Number of source units currently tracked. Should match the count of keys in the 'files' object; divergence indicates corruption.
parameter_definitions.files: The core payload: a mapping of source file paths to their metadata. Each entry includes content hash, file kind, indexing timestamp, and links to documentation files.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.contentHash: SHA-256 hex digest of the file content. Used to detect modifications without re-reading the entire file.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.fileKind: Categorizes the file (e.g., 'source', 'test'). Influences which analysis pipelines are applied.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.lastIndexedAt: Timestamp of when this file was last indexed. Used to decide if re-indexing is necessary.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.docs: Array of documentation entries for this file. Each entry specifies a locale and a path to the markdown document in the Atlas.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.sourceExists: Boolean indicating whether the source file is still present on disk. Allows the system to gracefully handle deleted files.

English.stability_and_risks: This manifest is the single source of truth for the indexing subsystem. If it becomes inconsistent (e.g., contentHash doesn't match actual file content), analysis and fix operations may produce false positives or miss violations. Deleting or corrupting this file will cause a full re-index on next sync, which can be time-consuming. The 'reverseIndexComplete' flag must be handled carefully: if set to false, view generation and certain queries will be degraded. The integrity of the entire system depends on this manifest staying in sync with the file system.

Simplified Chinese.role: 此清单文件是 ArchSpine Atlas 系统的中央索引，记录了所有已索引的源文件及其内容指纹，以及各语言环境下的文档链接。同时包含同步检查点数据，供运行时判断是否需要重新扫描。

parameter_definitions.schemaVersion: 定义清单模式的版本号。升级此版本可能破坏与旧工具的兼容性。
parameter_definitions.generatorVersion: 标识创建此清单的 ArchSpine 工具精确版本，便于调试和迁移。
parameter_definitions.createdAt: 初始创建时间戳，用于追踪索引的生命周期。
parameter_definitions.updatedAt: 最后修改时间戳，用于检测状态是否过时。
parameter_definitions.sync: 包含同步元数据的块，控制运行时是否认为索引是最新的，或需要完全重建。
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncAt: 记录上次同步操作完成的时间，用于增量同步决策。
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncMode: 指示上次同步模式为“完整”或“增量”，影响索引的可信度。
parameter_definitions.sync.reverseIndexComplete: 布尔标志，告知系统反向索引（从文档映射回源文件）是否已完全构建。若为 false，某些操作可能受损。
parameter_definitions.sync.indexedUnitCount: 当前跟踪的源单元数量，应与 files 对象中的键数量一致；不一致表示数据损坏。
parameter_definitions.files: 核心内容：源文件路径到元数据的映射。每个条目包含内容哈希、文件类型、索引时间戳及文档链接。
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.contentHash: 文件内容的 SHA-256 十六进制摘要，用于在不重新读取整个文件的情况下检测修改。
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.fileKind: 文件分类（如“source”、“test”），影响所应用的分析流程。
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.lastIndexedAt: 该文件上次被索引的时间戳，用于判断是否需要重新索引。
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.docs: 该文件的文档条目数组，每个条目指定语言环境和 Atlas 中的 Markdown 文档路径。
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.sourceExists: 布尔值，指示源文件是否仍存在于磁盘上，使系统能够优雅处理已删除的文件。

Simplified Chinese.stability_and_risks: 此清单是指纹子系统的唯一真实数据源。如果它变得不一致（例如 contentHash 与实际文件内容不匹配），分析和修复操作可能会产生误报或遗漏违规。删除或损坏此文件将导致下次同步时进行完全重新索引，这可能非常耗时。 reverseIndexComplete 标志必须谨慎处理：如果设置为 false，视图生成和某些查询将降级。整个系统的完整性依赖于此清单与文件系统保持同步。
